# Sourdough

_Shared components for dbt Labs UIs_

## Documentation

- [Browse components](https://ui.getdbt.com/)
- [Contribution guidelines](https://ui.getdbt.com/?path=/story/docs-contributing--page)

## Running Locally

_Use this guide if you're contributing or fixing bugs._

Make sure you are using the same node version found in `.nvmrc` and `.tool-versions`. This can be installed/configured via `asdf` or `nvm`. Install instructions can be found here:

https://www.macstadium.com/blog/install-asdf-on-macos

https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm#installing-and-updating

```sh
nvm use
pnpm i
pnpm start
```

Or if using `asdf`

```sh
asdf plugin-add pnpm
asdf plugin-add nodejs
asdf install
pnpm i
```

After building, a tab will open with Storybook. [Welcome to sourdough](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D8zlUUrFK-M) [jurassic park theme song plays]

## Linking local `sourdough` to consumer apps/modules

_Use this guide if you're contributing and want to run cloud-ui, dbt-explorer, dbt-dag with a local copy of sourdough_

Make sure the `bakery` repo is in the same parent directory as your app/module.
The default is `/Users/{username}/git` and will be set as `$GIT_PROJECTS_WORKDIR` during [Engineering Setup](https://www.notion.so/dbtlabs/Engineering-Setup-9e9058bbfda94f61bf8d0e43fe166a30#202ca25ed6b44a6b931cdb35770f8a84). Then run:

```sh
cd path/to/bakery
pnpm local-dev
```

See [local-dev/README.md](./scripts/local-dev/README.md) for more details, troubleshooting tips etc.

## Installation

_Use this guide if you're looking to use sourdough in your project._

The sourdough package is privately hosted on Github Packages. To Install locally you'll need to login with your github credentials, specifically with a personal access token.

### 1. Create a Personal Access Token with Github

> You may have already done this as part of setting up `fsh`, if so, you may be able to add the `read_packages` permission to your existing permission.

### 2. Set your ENV variable

Update your shell config file of choice and set/export your token as `$GITHUB_TOKEN`.

### 3. Add an .npmrc file to your repository

It should go in the root and contain:

```
@dbt-labs:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}
```

Eaxmple: [cloud-ui .npmrc file](https://github.com/dbt-labs/cloud-ui/blob/513dc5ed616f5f756b5f301001709acdde62960a/.npmrc)

### 4. Install @dbt-labs/sourdough

`(p)npm i --save @dbt-labs/sourdough`

### 5. (Optional) Use the sourdough starter tailwind theme

(`tailwind.config.js`)

```typescript
const defaultTheme = require('tailwindcss/defaultTheme');
const sourdoughTheme = require('@dbt-labs/sourdough/tailwind.config');

module.exports = {
  ...sourdoughTheme,
  content: [
    './src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx,mdx}',
    './public/index.html',
    // Add sourdough to the purge to ensure all the css is loaded.
    './node_modules/@dbt-labs/sourdough/dist/sourdough.js',
  ],
};
```

### 6. (Optional, if using devspaces) Add `GITHUB_TOKEN` to your devspace image

Devspace needs access to download our packages. In order to allow that access you must pass your github token through to your image.

This can be done by passing it through `buildArgs` to the image at build time.

1. Update the devspace manifest for the app that requires sourdough to include an environment variable for `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
   - Example: [cloud-ui devspace manifest](https://github.com/dbt-labs/fsh/blob/ece8a05e8004bd18d469c6096c3ee823b3d9ef57/pkg/template/devspace.yaml#L155)
2. Update helm-charts to pass this through to your app
   - Example: [cloud-ui devspace helm chart](- Example: [cloud-ui](https://github.com/dbt-labs/helm-charts/blob/4ff94273fbf8114b8c6a44088c9e0dca9ddf3d00/devspace/dbt-cloud-ui-next/devspace.yaml#L23-L25)
3. Update your app's Dockerfile to accept an ARG, copy the .npmrc from your local system to the image, `pnpm install`, and finally remove the .npmrc file.
   - Example: [cloud-ui Dockerfile](https://github.com/dbt-labs/cloud-ui/blob/ddbb83561614de9b94d2f78f9d7fcb6731315ec8/Dockerfile#L2-L15)

### 7. Add the FA_ASSISTANT_PAT_TOKEN to your github actions workflow

Now that you're using sourdough, your CI pipeline will need access to our package. Add an environment variable to your workflow of `GITHUB_TOKEN` and set it to `{{ secrets.FA_ASSISTANT_PAT_TOKEN }}` to allow it to install from our private registry.

Example: [cloud-ui GitHub action workflow](https://github.com/dbt-labs/cloud-ui/blob/513dc5ed616f5f756b5f301001709acdde62960a/.github/workflows/main.yml#L123)

### 8. Start using sourdough components

`import { Card } from '@dbt-labs/sourdough';`
